skills.name: nextjs
skills.rationale: App Router conventions, Server Components, layouts, data fetching, and deployment patterns. Core framework guidance that custom rules alone cannot fully cover.

skills.name: react-best-practices
skills.rationale: TSX quality checks for component structure, hooks usage, accessibility, performance, and TypeScript patterns. Complements our frontend-patterns rule with deeper analysis.

skills.name: shadcn
skills.rationale: Component installation, composition patterns, theming, and Tailwind integration for shadcn/ui. Prevents incorrect manual component creation when CLI can scaffold correctly.

skills.name: verification
skills.rationale: End-to-end flow verification: browser → API → data → response. Used after feature changes to confirm the full signal path works.

skills.name: investigation-mode
skills.rationale: Structured debugging triage: runtime logs → workflow status → browser verify → deploy/env. Triggers on frustration signals when observability checks fail.

skills.name: observability
skills.rationale: Instrumentation, monitoring dashboards, OpenTelemetry, and performance debugging. Extends our custom observability skill with platform-level insights.

skills.name: workflow
skills.rationale: Durable stepwise orchestration patterns. Informs the orchestrator skill's phase model and retry/resume design.

skills.name: vercel-cli
skills.rationale: Deployment commands, environment variable management, and project linking from terminal. Useful for CI/CD and deploy workflows.

custom_skills_complement: Custom skills cover Signal Lab-specific concerns (scenario observability verification, story execution workflow, submission evidence mapping, PRD orchestration) that no marketplace skill addresses. Marketplace skills provide general framework and platform best practices.
